prs.title: Add ER diagram to README
prs.body: Added ER diagram for database design with detailed entity relationships.

prs.title: docs: improve UI with modern floating cards content for software inte…
prs.body:
Redesigned the Software Interfaces section for better user engagement.
What's New:

Human-Centric Copy: Rewrote the section to be more welcoming ("We speak your language").

Modern UI: Replaced the static list with floating, shadow-backed cards using CSS grid.

Interactive Hover: Added smooth elevation effects for a premium feel.

Brand Iconography: Fixed R-project icons and ensured consistent branding.

Verified locally using a custom mkdocs setup.

prs.title: Fix #1284: Adjusted responsive breakpoint to 1280px for Nest Hub MaxFix #1284: Responsive breakpoint 1280px for Nest Hub Max
prs.body:
I have updated the CSS breakpoint to 1280px to fix the responsiveness issue specifically for devices like Nest Hub Max.

Changes made in material.css:
- Updated media query breakpoint to 1280px.
- Hidden the sidebar (.sidebar) for this resolution and below.
- Enabled the hamburger menu (.navbar-toggle) for navigation.

This ensures that high-resolution tablets/displays don't see a broken sidebar layout.

prs.title: Add Minecraft ServerHub API to README
prs.body:
This PR adds the Minecraft ServerHub API to the Games & Comics section. It provides real-time Minecraft server status, player counts, and MOTD without requiring an API key.

The entry has been formatted according to the details provided by the maintainer (@DRAGINOXd) in issue #5270.

Metadata:

Auth: No

HTTPS: Yes

CORS: Yes

prs.title: Add Minecraft ServerHub API to README
prs.body: Added the Minecraft ServerHub API to the Games & Comics category. The API provides Minecraft server status, player counts, and live status badges without requiring an API key.

prs.title: Add quick start guide for beginners
prs.body:
## Change
Added a beginner-friendly quick start section in docs README.

This helps new contributors:
- clone the repository  
- install dependencies  
- run a basic check  

## Why
Many beginners may not know the first setup steps.  
This section makes onboarding easier.

issues.title: [UX/UI] Sidebar responsiveness gap on intermediate screens (1280px)
issues.body:
Hi OpenML Team,

While testing the portal, I noticed that the sidebar behavior is inconsistent on intermediate screen sizes like Nest Hub Max (1280px).

On mobile (iPhone SE), the sidebar correctly hides into a hamburger menu.

On 1280px, the sidebar remains fixed and takes up significant screen real estate.

Proposal: I suggest enabling the toggle/minify functionality for the 1280px resolution as well, so users can expand their workspace when needed.

I have intermediate skills in HTML, CSS, and JS and would like to work on this fix. Please assign this to me
